title: Fixing Broken HTML Table Structure with Invalid Headings

statement.markdown: Fix the `tableStructureValidator` function to correctly validate HTML table structure. The function should return `true` if the table structure is valid and `false` otherwise. The table structure is valid if all table cells (`<td>`) have a corresponding table row (`<tr>`), and if all table rows have a corresponding table header cell (`<th>`) or data cell (`<td>`). However, if the table header cell has a `scope` attribute with value other than `row` or `col`, the table structure is invalid.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the HTML table structure as a string of opening and closing tags.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the table structure is valid or not.
statement.notes: The input string will only contain the table structure, without any additional HTML elements. The function should not modify the input string in any way.

constraints.description: The number of characters in the input string

examples.explanation: The table structure is valid because all table cells have a corresponding table row, and all table rows have a corresponding table header cell or data cell.
The table structure is invalid because the last table row is empty and does not have a corresponding table header cell or data cell.
The table structure is valid because all table cells have a corresponding table row, and all table rows have a corresponding table header cell or data cell.

hints.text: First hint text: Start by parsing the table structure into a data structure that you can easily traverse and validate.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Parse the table structure into a stack-based data structure, and then traverse the structure to validate it.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Iterate through each character in the input string and check if it corresponds to a valid table structure. This approach has a time complexity of O(n^2) and is not efficient.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution using a stack-based data structure has a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Parse the table structure into a stack-based data structure, and then traverse the structure to validate it.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The optimal solution correctly validates the table structure by checking all table cells and rows.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Parse the input string into a stack-based data structure, and then traverse the structure to validate it.
